title: A Difficult Negotiation
article_text: My boss made an assumption that I would accept a transfer to work elsewhere. I was truly disappointed because the new job would require a strict diet and a sharp schedule. I used to be more competitive, but now I only wanted to show some initiative. The gray office and plastic furniture made me feel sad. I knew I had to use discipline to not complicate the situation. During the negotiation, I spoke forth with my concerns about the passage of time. The motion of the clock on the wall seemed to freeze. My reputation was at stake, so I did not want to bother anyone. The extreme pressure taught me a lesson about the mechanism of office politics. I decided to qualify my words and not disappoint my team.
